medicines.description: Pure dextroenantiomer of medetomidine; twice as potent as the racemic mixture.

canine.notes: 1-10 µg/kg i.v., i.m., or s.c.. 125 µg/m² gel applied to oral mucosa for noise anxiety.

feline.notes: 1-10 µg/kg i.v., i.m., or s.c..

medicines.onset_of_action: Rapid; duration of analgesia is approximately 1 hour at 5 µg/kg.
drug_interactions: Significantly reduces dose requirements of concurrently administered anaesthetic drugs.

medicines.description: Alpha-2 adrenoreceptor agonist providing dose-dependent sedation and muscle relaxation.

canine.notes: 5-20 µg/kg i.v., i.m., or s.c.. Use lower end for i.v.

feline.notes: 5-20 µg/kg i.v., i.m., or s.c..

drug_interactions: Potentiates effects of other anaesthetic agents.
Do not use with sympathomimetic amines.

medicines.description: Selective alpha-2 adrenoreceptor antagonist used to reverse effects of alpha-2 agonists.
indications: Reversal of sedation and cardiovascular effects of medetomidine or dexmedetomidine.

canine.notes: 5x previous medetomidine dose or 10x dexmedetomidine dose i.m.. Use 100 µg/kg if dose unknown.

feline.notes: 2.5x previous medetomidine dose or 5x dexmedetomidine dose i.m..

drug_interactions: Antagonizes all alpha-2 agonists.

medicines.description: Analgesic with poor anti-inflammatory effects; mechanism involving central prostaglandin inhibition.
indications: Control of mild to moderate pain

canine.notes: q8h. Oral Pardale-V (with codeine) dose is 1 tab/12kg q8h.

feline.notes: DO NOT USE. Extremely toxic to cats.

drug_interactions: Metoclopramide enhances absorption.

medicines.description: Antimuscarinic agent blocking acetylcholine at parasympathetic nerve terminals.

canine.notes: 0.01-0.03 mg/kg i.v. for bradycardia. 0.2-0.5 mg/kg for OP poisoning.

feline.notes: 0.01-0.03 mg/kg i.v. for bradycardia.

drug_interactions: Combination with alpha-2 agonists not recommended.
Potentiated by antihistamines and pethidine.

medicines.description: Quaternary antimuscarinic agent without central effects as it does not cross the BBB.

canine.notes: 2-10 µg/kg i.v., i.m. for bradyarrhythmias.

feline.notes: 2-10 µg/kg i.v., i.m. for bradyarrhythmias.

contraindications: None specified; use caution in cases where tachycardia is dangerous.
drug_interactions: Potentiated by benzodiazepines and phenothiazines.
Antagonizes metoclopramide effects.

medicines.description: Benzodiazepine that enhances GABA activity in the CNS.

canine.notes: Bolus i.v. or intrarectally for seizures; repeat every 10 min up to 3 times.

feline.notes: 0.1-0.2 mg/kg i.v. once for appetite. Seizure doses as for dogs.

side_effects: Fulminant hepatic necrosis in cats (repeated oral use)
drug_interactions: Cimetidine inhibits metabolism.
Potentiates sedative effects of opioids.

medicines.description: Short-acting benzodiazepine; more potent and less irritant than diazepam.

canine.notes: i.v. or intrarectally. CRI for status epilepticus at 0.3 mg/kg/h.

feline.notes: i.v. or intrarectally.

drug_interactions: Erythromycin inhibits metabolism.
Potentiates effects of propofol and inhalants.

medicines.description: Specific benzodiazepine receptor antagonist.
indications: Reversal of benzodiazepine-induced sedation and respiratory depression.

canine.notes: i.v. slowly. Duration of action is short (~1 hour).

feline.notes: i.v. slowly.

contraindications: Patients suspected of tricyclic antidepressant overdose (risk of seizures).
side_effects: May trigger seizures in animals on long-term benzodiazepines.
drug_interactions: Specific antagonist to diazepam, midazolam, etc.

medicines.description: Potent volatile anaesthetic with rapid induction and recovery.
indications: Induction and maintenance of general anaesthesia.

canine.notes: Minimum Alveolar Concentration (MAC) is ~1.2%. Deliver via calibrated vaporizer.

feline.notes: MAC is ~1.6% in cats.

drug_interactions: Opioids and nitrous oxide reduce required concentration.

medicines.description: Volatile anaesthetic with even faster induction/recovery than isoflurane and less pungent smell.
indications: Induction and maintenance of general anaesthesia.

canine.notes: MAC is ~2.5%. 6-8% required for induction in unpremedicated dogs.

feline.notes: MAC is similar (~2.5%).

drug_interactions: Nitrous oxide and opioids reduce required concentration.

medicines.description: Neurosteroid injectable anesthetic agent.

canine.notes: 3 mg/kg i.v. (unpremedicated), 2 mg/kg (premedicated). CRI at 6-9 mg/kg/h.

feline.notes: 2-5 mg/kg i.v. for induction. CRI at 7-10 mg/kg/h.

contraindications: Do not combine with other i.v. anaesthetic agents.
drug_interactions: Premedication reduces required induction dose.

medicines.description: Dissociative anesthetic and NMDA receptor antagonist.
indications: NMDA receptor blockade to inhibit central sensitization

canine.notes: 2 mg/kg i.v. for induction (with diazepam). 5-7 mg/kg i.m. with medetomidine.

feline.notes: 2.5-7.5 mg/kg i.m. (depending on combination and procedure).

drug_interactions: Combination with alpha-2 agonists requires delayed reversal (wait 45 min).

medicines.description: Short-acting lipophilic anesthetic; GABA modulator.

canine.notes: 6-7 mg/kg i.v. (unpremedicated); 1-4 mg/kg (premedicated). CRI at 0.1-0.4 mg/kg/min.

feline.notes: 8 mg/kg i.v. (unpremedicated); 2-5 mg/kg (premedicated).

contraindications: None specific, but use caution in hypovolaemic patients.
side_effects: Heinz body anaemia (reported in cats with repeated doses)
drug_interactions: No major clinical interactions; premedication reduces dose.

medicines.description: Local anaesthetic and Class 1b antiarrhythmic agent.

canine.notes: 2-8 mg/kg i.v. boluses for arrhythmias. Max local dose 4 mg/kg.

feline.notes: 0.25-2.0 mg/kg i.v. slowly for arrhythmias. Highly sensitive to toxicity.

contraindications: Do not give by CRI to cats (negative haemodynamic effects)
Do not use with adrenaline for ring blocks
drug_interactions: Cimetidine/propranolol may prolong clearance.

medicines.description: Preferential COX-2 inhibitor; limiting inflammatory prostaglandins.

canine.notes: i.v., s.c. or p.o. (single or divided). Maintenance dose may be reduced to 2 mg/kg.

feline.notes: Single perioperative dose only. Tablets not authorized for cats.

side_effects: Renal papillary necrosis (risk in hypotensive patients)
drug_interactions: Do not combine with other NSAIDs or glucocorticoids.

medicines.description: Non-selective COX inhibitor and lipoxygenase inhibitor.

canine.notes: 2 mg/kg s.c./i.m./i.v. for 3 days; 0.25 mg/kg p.o. for long-term.

feline.notes: 2 mg/kg s.c. for 3 days; 1 mg/kg p.o. for up to 5 days.

drug_interactions: Do not use with other NSAIDs or corticosteroids.

medicines.description: Selective COX-2 inhibitor.

canine.notes: p.o. q24h. Give 2 hours before surgery for perioperative use.

feline.notes: DO NOT USE in cats.

drug_interactions: Potential synergy with platinum chemotherapy for bladder tumours.

medicines.description: Preferential COX-2 inhibitor.

canine.notes: 0.2 mg/kg initial dose; 0.1 mg/kg maintenance p.o. q24h.

feline.notes: 0.3 mg/kg s.c. once for surgery. Chronic pain: 0.1 mg/kg q24h then 0.05 mg/kg.

drug_interactions: Do not combine with other NSAIDs or glucocorticoids.

medicines.description: Selective COX-2 inhibitor; tissue selective (concentrates at inflammation sites).

canine.notes: p.o. q24h (do not give with food). 2 mg/kg s.c. for surgery.

feline.notes: p.o. q24h (highly palatable). 2 mg/kg s.c. for surgery.

medicines.onset_of_action: Short plasma half-life (~2h), but concentrates in tissues.
drug_interactions: Do not combine with other NSAIDs or glucocorticoids.

medicines.description: Partial mu agonist; high affinity and slow dissociation from receptors.
indications: Relief of mild to moderate perioperative pain

canine.notes: i.v., i.m., or s.c. q6h.

feline.notes: i.v., i.m., s.c., or oral transmucosal (OTM).

contraindications: None specific, but may antagonize full mu agonists.
drug_interactions: Reduces doses of induction anaesthetics.

medicines.description: Kappa agonist and mu antagonist.

canine.notes: 0.2-0.5 mg/kg for analgesia. 0.05-0.1 mg/kg i.v./i.m. for cough.

feline.notes: i.v., i.m., or s.c. for analgesia.

contraindications: Lower respiratory disease with copious mucus
Not recommended if full mu analgesia is needed during surgery
drug_interactions: Antagonizes analgesia of full mu agonists (morphine, fentanyl).

medicines.description: Pure mu agonist; 50-100 times more potent than morphine.

canine.notes: CRI at 2.5-10 µg/kg/h during anaesthesia. Patch: 4 µg/kg/h.

feline.notes: 5 µg/kg boluses. 25 µg/h patch for 3-5 kg cat.

medicines.onset_of_action: Immediate (i.v.); Patches take 7h (cats) or 24h (dogs) to act.
drug_interactions: Reduces required doses of other anaesthetics.

medicines.description: Pure mu agonist; synthetic opioid similar to morphine.

canine.notes: i.m. or 0.1-0.3 mg/kg i.v. as required.

feline.notes: i.m. or i.v.. 0.6 mg/kg for OTM.

drug_interactions: Do not use with MAOIs (risk of hyperpyrexia).

medicines.description: Pure mu agonist; the reference opioid.

canine.notes: 0.5 mg/kg i.m. q2h for analgesia. Epidural: 0.1-0.2 mg/kg (preservative-free).

feline.notes: i.v. or i.m. q3-4h.

contraindications: Cases where histamine release is dangerous
drug_interactions: Potentiated by antihistamines and phenothiazines.

medicines.description: Multimodal analgesic: mu agonist and inhibitor of noradrenaline/5-HT reuptake.
indications: Mild acute and chronic soft tissue/musculoskeletal pain

canine.notes: p.o. q8h. 2 mg/kg i.v. for acute pain.

feline.notes: 2-4 mg/kg p.o. q8h. 1-2 mg/kg i.v./s.c.

drug_interactions: Risk of Serotonin Syndrome with SSRIs/MAOIs.
Effectiveness reduced by ondansetron.

medicines.description: Competitive opioid receptor antagonist.
indications: Management of unexpectedly long recovery from anaesthesia with opioids.

canine.notes: 0.01-0.02 mg/kg i.v. or 0.04 mg/kg i.m./s.c.

feline.notes: 0.01-0.02 mg/kg i.v. or 0.04 mg/kg i.m./s.c.

side_effects: Antanalgesia (sudden onset of severe pain if used after major surgery).
drug_interactions: Specific antagonist for mu, kappa, and delta opioid receptors.

medicines.description: Potent catecholamine acting on alpha and beta-adrenergic receptors.

canine.notes: 10 µg/kg i.v. every 3-5 min for CPA. Double dose if used intratreacheally.

feline.notes: 10 µg/kg i.v. or i.m. for anaphylaxis.

drug_interactions: Halothane sensitizes myocardium to arrhythmias.
Beta-blockers (propranolol) may block beta effects causing severe hypertension.

medicines.description: Phenothiazine CNS depressant causing sedation and reduction in activity.

canine.notes: 0.01-0.05 mg/kg i.m.. 0.005-0.01 mg/kg for Boxers (risk of sinoatrial block).

feline.notes: 0.01-0.05 mg/kg i.m., s.c. or i.v. (slow).

side_effects: Red blood cell sequestration in spleen
drug_interactions: Additive CNS depression with barbiturates or propofol.
Concomitant adrenaline may cause tachycardia/vasodilation.

medicines.description: Synthetic direct-acting beta-1 adrenergic agonist.
indications: Short-term inotropic support for heart failure (DCM)

canine.notes: 2.5-20 µg/kg/min CRI. Titrate to effect.

feline.notes: 1-5 µg/kg/min CRI. Higher doses may cause seizures in cats.

drug_interactions: Beta-blockers antagonize effects.
Halothane increases arrhythmia risk.
